name: Immix Biopharma, Inc.
business: Immix Biopharma, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on the application of chimeric antigen receptor cell therapy (CAR-T) in light chain (AL) Amyloidosis and other serious diseases. Our lead cell therapy candidate is CAR-T NXC-201, currently being evaluated in our ongoing United States Phase 1b/2 NEXICART-2 clinical trial and an ex-U.S. phase 1b/2a NEXICART-1 clinical trial.

exposures.sentence: Immix Biopharma, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on the application of chimeric antigen receptor cell therapy (CAR-T) in light chain (AL) Amyloidosis and other serious diseases.

catalysts.event: Positive interim phase 2 safety and efficacy data from NEXICART-2 announced at ASH 2025
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we announced positive interim phase 2 safety and efficacy data from our Phase1/2 NEXICART-2 clinical trial, the first U.S. trial of CAR-T in relapsed/refractory light chain (AL) amyloidosis, evaluating NXC-201.

catalysts.event: FDA granted Breakthrough Therapy designation for NXC-201
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, the FDA granted Breakthrough Therapy designation to sterically-optimized CAR-T NXC-201 for the treatment of relapsed/refractory AL amyloidosis.

catalysts.event: FDA granted RMAT designation for NXC-201
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, the FDA granted Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation to sterically-optimized CAR-T NXC-201 for the treatment of relapsed/refractory AL amyloidosis.

catalysts.event: NXC-201 clinical data presented at ASCO 2025
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, NXC-201 clinical data in relapsed/refractory AL Amyloidosis was presented in an oral presentation at the 2025 American Society of Clinical Oncology Annual Meeting (ASCO 2025).

On July 25, 2024, we were awarded an $8 million grant from the California Institute for Regenerative Medicine (CIRM) to support the clinical development of chimeric antigen receptor T-cell therapy NXC-201 for the treatment of relapsed/refractory AL Amyloidosis.

catalysts.event: Public offering of common stock and pre-funded warrants
catalysts.sentence: On December 7, 2025, we entered into an underwriting agreement (the 2025 Underwriting Agreement) with Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC (Morgan Stanley), as representative of the several underwriters named in Schedule 1 thereto, relating to the issuance and sale (the 2025 Offering) of 19,117,646 shares (the Shares) of our common stock, and pre-funded warrants to purchase up to 490,196 shares of our common stock (the Pre-Funded Warrants).

On September 5, 2025 and September 11, 2025, we entered into Securities Purchase Agreements (the September 2025 Securities Purchase Agreements) and Registration Rights Agreements with certain accredited investors (the Purchasers), pursuant to which we sold to the Purchasers in a private placement transaction (the Private Placement) (i) an aggregate of 3,915,604 shares of common stock and (ii) non-transferable warrants to purchase up to an aggregate of 2,936,709 shares of common stock (the Warrants).

tamClaims.claim: Global amyloidosis treatment market size estimated at $5.80 billion in 2024, projected to reach $11.13 billion by 2033, CAGR 7.5%
tamClaims.sentence: The global amyloidosis treatment market size was estimated at $5.80 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach $11.13 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 7.5% from 2025 to 2033, according to Grand View Research.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. observed prevalence of relapsed/refractory AL Amyloidosis estimated to reach 38,500 patients in 2026
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S. observed prevalence of relapsed/refractory AL Amyloidosis is growing 12% per year according to Staron, et al Blood Cancer Journal, estimated to reach 38,500 patients in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: AL amyloidosis one-year mortality rate of 47%, 76% caused by cardiac amyloidosis
tamClaims.sentence: AL amyloidosis has a one-year mortality rate of 47%, 76% of which is caused by cardiac amyloidosis, according to Alexion.
